name: Dungan

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Dungan (dung1253).

notes: Skeleton. Dungan is a Sinitic (Sino-Tibetan) language — a Mandarin variety of the Central Asian Dungan people — written in a Cyrillic alphabet with the extra letters ә, ў, ү, ң and җ. Letters are mapped to approximate phonemic values (aspirated/unaspirated stop pairs б/п, г/к, д/т; retroflex and alveolo-palatal series). The three-to-four lexical tones are not marked in the orthography and are left unencoded, so an elevated PER floor is expected. Contextual e/je and o/jo readings are only approximately covered.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Dungan (dung1253)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
